why_this_recipient: Rizio-Hamilton is Co-Head of Securities Litigation at BLBG — the most senior correct contact at the firm most aligned with this matter type. BLBG has the largest auditor and financial institution gatekeeper recovery track record of any firm on the list.
why_now: The matter's gatekeeper exposure pattern maps to BLBG's most cited recovery structures, including the EY Lehman $99M settlement and BankAmerica $2.425B recovery.
practice_match: Auditor liability, underwriter/bank gatekeeper liability, securities fraud — highest match in batch.
economic_match: BLBG operates on contingency for institutional plaintiff work. Matter economics align.
conflict_screen_note: BLBG is plaintiff-only firm. No defense conflict risk identified.

v1_issues: 'Professional failure enabled the fraud' — premature legal conclusion
v2_changes: Language corrected to 'role may warrant scrutiny'
Added economic seriousness line and 'early resolution preferred' framing

outreach_draft.subject: Post-Judgment Gatekeeper Recovery — Offshore Fraud Judgment — Financial Institution Exposure Assessment
outreach_draft.body:
Dear Mr. Rizio-Hamilton,

I am writing regarding a post-judgment recovery matter that may align with Bernstein Litowitz's financial institution and auditor liability practice.

Our client holds an unsatisfied fraud judgment against an offshore foreign corporation. Direct enforcement against the debtor is not viable. The recovery path we are assessing involves potential claims or leverage against non-debtor gatekeepers — financial institutions and related professional advisors whose role in the transaction or related conduct may warrant scrutiny.

The immediate objective is to assess whether viable recovery may be pursued against non-debtor gatekeepers, with early negotiated resolution preferred where feasible. The matter is only of interest if counsel sees a realistic path to material recovery against solvent non-debtor actors.

Bernstein Litowitz's record in financial institution accountability — the $2.425B BankAmerica recovery, the $150M JPMorgan settlement, and your firm's sustained track record in auditor liability matters — reflects the most directly relevant experience with this recovery structure of any firm I am approaching.

I would welcome a brief preliminary conversation to assess fit. No commitment implied.

Kind regards,

Grant de Graf
CorporateXfactor
Special Situations / Post-Judgment Recovery

Website: corporatexfactor.com
Tel 212-444-1813
